title: Tyson CEO King tells Congress to blame 'basic economics' for high beef prices, not Tyson
text: Tyson Foods Inc. TSN, -0.97% Chief Executive Donnie King said beef prices aren't rising because the packaged meats company just decided to raise prices, but because of "market forces" including strong demand, COVID-19-related labor shortages that constrained production and record inflation. "Tyson does not set the prices for either the cattle we buy or the beer our customers purchase," King said, in prepared testimony to Congress ahead of a U.S. House Agriculture Committee meeting scheduled for Wednesday.

title: Tyson Foods CEO: Beef Prices Driven by Consistently Strong Demand, Constrained Production and Record Inflation
text: “It's a question of basic supply and demand,” says King “It's a question of basic supply and demand,” says King

title: Tyson Foods to offer free education to all U.S. workers
text: Tyson Foods Inc. TSN, -0.43% has announced it will offer free education to all U.S. workers starting this summer, including master's and associate's degrees, professional certificates and technology fundamentals. The $60 million investment will span 175 programs across 35 universities and education providers with books, tuition and fees included through a partnership with Guild, an education platform.

title: Tyson Foods Announces Second Quarter Earnings Conference Call and Webcast
text: SPRINGDALE, Ark., April 06, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Tyson Foods, Inc. (NYSE: TSN) will release second quarter 2022 financial results on Monday, May 9, 2022. Management will host a conference call and webcast beginning at 9:00 a.m. Eastern Time (8:00 a.m. Central Time). A press release and supplemental materials will be issued before the market opens that morning.

title: What Makes Tyson (TSN) a New Strong Buy Stock
